title: There Is Confusion
description: There Is Confusion — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "There Is Confusion" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: "Aaron Bye"
memes.description: "A white Quaker slaveholder in late eighteenth-century Philadelphia in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, who freed his slaves in 1780 according to Pennsylvania law and whose family's relationship with the black Byes shapes the novel's multi-generational exploration of racial inheritance and obligation."

memes.label: "Alec Marshall"
memes.description: "Joanna Marshall's eldest brother in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, a steady, practical man who enters real estate and marries Helena Arnold after a decade of unspoken courtship."

memes.label: "Alice Graves"
memes.description: "The younger sister of Susan Graves, Alice Graves worked as a seamstress in New York City and became the wife of Meriwether Bye and mother of Peter Bye in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion."

memes.label: "Alice Talbert"
memes.description: "Daughter of a prominent Philadelphia lawyer and a member of the city's colored elite in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, whose chilly social exclusiveness toward Maggie Ellersley embodies the class tensions within the novel's African American community."

memes.label: "Arabelle Morton"
memes.description: "A young woman of Philadelphia's colored society whose good-natured shallowness and complete social assurance make her a fixture of Peter Bye's social circle and, later, a crucial sponsor of Maggie Ellersley's entrance into respectable society in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion."

memes.label: Baltimore, Maryland
memes.description: A major mid-Atlantic city in the United States, appearing in *There Is Confusion* as a center for Maggie Ellersley's business operations and part of Peter Bye's regional network.

memes.label: "Brian Spencer"
memes.description: "Sylvia Marshall's steady, devoted suitor and eventual husband in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, a college-educated man who partners with Alec Marshall in real estate and provides the novel's most incisive analysis of Peter Bye's color complex."

memes.label: "Church Life in There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "The role of the African Methodist Episcopal Church and religious community as institutional anchors in There Is Confusion."

memes.label: Civil War Legacy
memes.description: The generational impact of the Civil War on African American families and communities in There Is Confusion, shaping identities through military service, loss, and the unfinished promise of freedom.

memes.label: Concept Index
memes.description: Thematic and conceptual index for "There Is Confusion" — covering social, cultural, and historical themes explored in the novel.

memes.label: The Concert in There Is Confusion
memes.description: Concerts, recitals, and benefit performances in Jessie Redmon Fauset's *There Is Confusion* function as the primary arena for Joanna Marshall's artistic ambition — sites where talent, race, gender, and professional aspiration intersect, and where the novel's exploration of Black artistic achievement in early twentieth-century America reaches its fullest expression.

memes.label: "Courtship in There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "Courtship customs, romantic expectations, and gender dynamics in the social world of There Is Confusion."

memes.label: Dance Culture in There Is Confusion
memes.description: Dance as artistic expression, social ritual, and professional aspiration in Jessie Redmon Fauset's *There Is Confusion*, centered on Joanna Marshall's talent and the role of dance in Black middle-class life in early twentieth-century Philadelphia and New York.

memes.label: "Racial Discrimination in There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "Experiences of racial discrimination, exclusion, and prejudice as depicted in Fauset's There Is Confusion."

memes.label: "Dr. Meriwether Bye"
memes.description: "The white Quaker physician of the Bye family of Bryn Mawr, youngest son of Aaron and Dinah Bye, whose childhood association with Isaiah Bye and his family's role as slaveholding patrons shape the Bye naming legacy across three generations in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion."
aliases: Dr. Meriwether Bye of Bryn Mawr

memes.label: "Joanna's Easter Discovery"
memes.description: "The inciting event of There Is Confusion: on Easter Sunday, ten-year-old Joanna Marshall reveals her exceptional singing talent to Madame Caldwell, transforming her abstract ambition for greatness into a concrete artistic pursuit."

memes.label: "Education in There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "The role of schooling, self-improvement, and literacy as tools of racial empowerment in Jessie Redmon Fauset's There Is Confusion."

memes.label: Event Index
memes.description: Narrative and historical event index for "There Is Confusion" — key plot events, social gatherings, and historical moments.

memes.label: "Faith and Religious Belief in There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "The role of religious faith, spiritual resilience, and the church community in providing meaning and strength to the characters of There Is Confusion."

memes.label: Family in There Is Confusion
memes.description: A foundational theme of Jessie Redmon Fauset's *There Is Confusion* — family as lineage, burden, identity, and aspiration, traced through the three-generation saga of the Bye family, whose history of slavery, freedom, ambition, and failure shapes every character in the novel.

relations.label: Legacy of Slavery in There Is Confusion

memes.label: Featured Pages
memes.description: Curated selection of the most thoroughly developed pages in the "There Is Confusion" wiki, ranked by reference count.

memes.label: "Fifty-Ninth Street, New York"
memes.description: "The Marshall family home in Manhattan in There Is Confusion. The address signifies the family's achieved middle-class respectability and provides the domestic setting for Joanna's childhood aspirations."

memes.label: "The Grant Banquet"
memes.description: "A pivotal backstory event in There Is Confusion: Joel Marshall's catering of a banquet for President Ulysses S. Grant in Richmond, which establishes his national reputation and enables the family's migration to New York."

memes.label: Harlem
memes.description: The vibrant African American neighborhood in upper Manhattan that serves as the primary setting of There Is Confusion, representing both community aspiration and the complexities of urban Black life in the early twentieth century.

memes.label: "Harlem Renaissance Context of There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "There Is Confusion as a work of the Harlem Renaissance, exploring its literary and cultural positioning within the movement and Fauset's distinctive focus on middle-class African American life."

memes.label: "Harley Alexander"
memes.description: "A supporting character in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion — a dentist, former suitor of Vera Manning, and wartime companion of Peter Bye aboard the troopship to France during World War I."

memes.label: "Harry Portor"
memes.description: "A Harvard-educated doctor and close friend of the Marshall-Bye social circle in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, whose unrequited love for Joanna Marshall parallels the novel's broader themes of romantic misalignment."

memes.label: "Helena Arnold"
memes.description: "A dancer and the wife of Alexander 'Alec' Marshall in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, whose talent and connections through Vera Sharples bridge the worlds of African American art and white patronage."

memes.label: "Henderson Neal"
memes.description: "A mysterious gambler and cardsharp in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, boarder at Mrs. Ellersley's house who briefly marries Maggie Ellersley before dying in a street accident."

memes.label: "Isaiah Bye"
memes.description: "The son of Joshua Bye and Belle Potter, Isaiah Bye is an ancestor figure in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion — a self-educated black man who, spurred by a boyhood insult, founded a school for colored youth in Philadelphia and became a beloved leader in his community."

memes.label: "Jessie Redmon Fauset"
memes.description: "Jessie Redmon Fauset (1882–1961) was an African American novelist, poet, literary editor, and educator. As literary editor of The Crisis from 1919 to 1926, she was a central figure of the Harlem Renaissance. Her 1924 novel There Is Confusion is the primary corpus of this wiki."

memes.label: "Joanna Marshall"
memes.description: "Protagonist of Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, an ambitious young African American woman with a remarkable singing voice who pursues artistic greatness against racial and gender barriers."

memes.label: "Joel Marshall"
memes.description: "Father of Joanna Marshall in There Is Confusion. A formerly enslaved man who became a successful New York caterer, whose thwarted ambition for intellectual greatness is inherited and pursued by his daughter."

memes.label: "Joshua Bye"
memes.description: "The enslaved great-grandfather of Peter Bye in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, freed in 1780 by Quaker Aaron Bye, whose labor built the white Bye family fortune."

memes.label: "Judy Bye"
memes.description: "An enslaved woman and matriarch of the Bye family in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, whose fierce loyalty to the white Bye household and her own mysterious interiority establish the novel's meditation on slavery's multigenerational legacy."

memes.label: "Madame Caldwell"
memes.description: "A renowned African American singer in There Is Confusion who discovers Joanna Marshall's vocal talent and urges her father to train her voice."

memes.label: "Madame Harkness"
memes.description: "An African American entrepreneur and hair culturist in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, who invents a method of hair straightening and softening and employs Maggie Ellersley as supervisor of her branch stores in Philadelphia, Baltimore, and Washington, D.C."

memes.label: "Maggie Ellersley"
memes.description: "A young woman of humble origins in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, who seeks respectability and love while navigating class barriers — a friend of Sylvia Marshall and the love interest of Philip Marshall."

memes.label: Marriage in There Is Confusion
memes.description: A central thematic concern of Jessie Redmon Fauset's *There Is Confusion* — marriage as both a personal union and a social institution, explored through the intersecting love stories of Joanna Marshall, Peter Bye, and Maggie Ellersley, each navigating the intersections of love, ambition, class, and racial identity.

relations.label: Racial Ambition in There Is Confusion

memes.label: "The Medical Profession in There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "Medicine as aspiration, professional identity, and generational ambition in Jessie Redmon Fauset's There Is Confusion."

memes.label: Meriwether Bye
memes.description: A central figure in Jessie Redmon Fauset's *There Is Confusion* — the black Meriwether Bye, father of protagonist Peter Bye, whose failed ambitions and early death cast a long shadow over his son's life. Also the name of the young white Dr. Meriwether Bye, whose battlefield friendship with Peter offers a redemptive counterpoint.

relations.label: Racial Ambition in There Is Confusion

memes.label: "Mis' Sparrow"
memes.description: "A minor but symbolically significant character in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, the cousin and companion of Maggie Ellersley's mother, whose life of poverty and resentment illuminates the social conditions Maggie is desperate to escape."

memes.label: "Miss Ashby"
memes.description: "A white dancer and the acknowledged star of the Broadway production 'America' in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, whose professional jealousy of Joanna Marshall illustrates the racial hierarchies embedded in the early twentieth-century theater world."

memes.label: "Miss Mason"
memes.description: "A neighbor in the apartment building where Maggie and Henderson Neal live after their marriage, whose friendly curiosity and North Pennsylvania accent offer Maggie a rare moment of uncomplicated social warmth in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion."

memes.label: "Miss Phelps"
memes.description: "A member of the dance performance committee in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, whose enthusiastic response to Joanna Marshall's audition — 'Oh, but that's great, that's genius!' — marks the turning point in the committee's reception."

memes.label: "Miss Rosen"
memes.description: "Secretary of the dance performance committee in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, who helps arrange Joanna Marshall's audition before the Board."

memes.label: "Miss Shanley"
memes.description: "Peter Bye and Joanna Marshall's teacher at the Fifty-second Street school in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, whose classroom becomes the unlikely stage for Peter's revelatory anatomical recitation."

memes.label: "Miss Susan Graves"
memes.description: "The elder sister of Alice Graves and guardian of Peter Bye after Meriwether Bye's death, Miss Susan Graves is a proud member of the Graves family of Gravestown, New Jersey, who worked as a housekeeper while upholding strict standards of family lineage and social propriety in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion."

memes.label: "Mr. Hale"
memes.description: "A tall, dark theater manager in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion who takes a professional risk by hiring Joanna Marshall for the Broadway production 'America,' becoming the agent of her theatrical breakthrough."

memes.label: "Mr. Howe"
memes.description: "John Howe, a Lincoln University divinity student who boards with the Ellersley family, helps them escape their impoverished neighborhood, and later returns as the Reverend John Howe — a pivotal benefactor in Maggie Ellersley's early life in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion."

memes.label: "Mr. Simpson"
memes.description: "A lodger in the Ellersley boarding house in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, whose accidental negligence enables Neal's violent assault on Maggie Ellersley and whose subsequent remorse makes him her devoted protector."

memes.label: "Mrs. Ellersley"
memes.description: "Maggie Ellersley's widowed mother in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion — a hardworking laundress whose life of poverty and struggle shapes Maggie's determination to escape her circumstances through marriage and social ambition."

memes.label: "Mrs. Larrabee"
memes.description: "The landlady of Peter Bye's fraternity boarding house in West Philadelphia, whose warm rooms and friendly smile offer a backdrop to key moments in Peter's emotional life in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion."

memes.label: "Mrs. Lea"
memes.description: "A wealthy white socialite from Bryn Mawr, Pennsylvania, whose encounter with Peter Bye in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion exemplifies the casual racial prejudice that talented Black professionals faced in early twentieth-century America."

memes.label: "Mrs. Proctor"
memes.description: "The young bride of the son of the white family for whom Mis' Sparrow works, whose rise from obscure poverty to prosperous marriage becomes a focal point for Mis' Sparrow's resentment and Maggie Ellersley's reflection on class mobility in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion."

memes.label: "Mrs. Reading"
memes.description: "The landlady from whom Meriwether Bye rented rooms in New York, and who frequently detained the young Peter Bye as collateral against his father's unpaid bills — a minor figure whose practical resistance to Meriwether's irresponsibility illuminates the precariousness of Peter Bye's childhood in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion."

memes.label: "Mrs. Terry"
memes.description: "The colored worker in charge of the leave-center at Chambéry, France during World War I in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, under whom Maggie Ellersley serves and Peter Bye convalesces."

memes.label: "Music in There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "The role of music as artistic aspiration, racial advancement strategy, and cultural expression in Jessie Redmon Fauset's There Is Confusion."

memes.label: New York City
memes.description: The metropolitan setting that frames the aspirations and mobility of African American characters in There Is Confusion, contrasting with Philadelphia as a site of both greater opportunity and more complex racial dynamics.

memes.label: "Performance and Theater in There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "The role of theatrical performance, stage dancing, and artistic production as vehicles for identity and ambition in There Is Confusion."

memes.label: Person Index
memes.description: Complete index of all characters in "There Is Confusion" by Jessie Redmon Fauset, organized alphabetically with quality ratings.

memes.label: Peter Bye
memes.description: The male protagonist of Jessie Redmon Fauset's *There Is Confusion* — a gifted but directionless young Black man who must overcome the legacy of his father's failure to become a distinguished surgeon, and whose love for Joanna Marshall catalyzes his transformation.

relations.label: Racial Ambition in There Is Confusion

memes.label: Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
memes.description: The primary setting of Jessie Redmon Fauset's *There Is Confusion*, a city where the intertwined histories of the black and white Bye families unfold across generations — from the late eighteenth century through World War I. A landscape of racial ambition, social stratification, and Black middle-class aspiration.

relations.label: Racial Ambition in There Is Confusion

memes.label: "Philip Marshall"
memes.description: "Joanna Marshall's older brother in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, a Harvard-educated intellectual and aspiring race leader who marries Maggie Ellersley after years of missed connection and dies from war wounds."

memes.label: Place Index
memes.description: Geographic index for "There Is Confusion" — locations in Philadelphia, New York, and beyond that shape the novel's setting.

memes.label: "Poverty and Economic Hardship in There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "The experience of poverty, economic hardship, and working-class struggle in Jessie Redmon Fauset's There Is Confusion."

memes.label: "Racial Prejudice in There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "The depiction of systemic racial prejudice and its psychological, professional, and social effects on the characters of There Is Confusion."

memes.label: "Racial Ambition in There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "The central thematic concern of There Is Confusion: the multi-generational drive for personal greatness in the face of systemic racial barriers, explored through the Marshall family."

memes.label: "Racial Passing in There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "The theme of racial passing — the ability and choice to cross the color line — as it appears in Jessie Redmon Fauset's There Is Confusion."

memes.label: "Richmond, Virginia"
memes.description: "The birthplace of Joel Marshall and origin point of the Marshall family in There Is Confusion. Richmond represents the antebellum South and the legacy of slavery from which the family rises across generations."

memes.label: "Selma Morton"
memes.description: "The sister of Arabelle Morton, Selma Morton appears briefly in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion as an educated Black woman who experienced racial discrimination during her Master's degree graduation ceremony."

memes.label: "Legacy of Slavery in There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "The multi-generational impact of American slavery as depicted through the Marshall family in Fauset's novel, from Joel's enslaved childhood to Joanna's artistic aspirations."

memes.label: "Social Class in There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "The role of social class distinctions, the 'better class of colored people,' and class consciousness in Fauset's depiction of early twentieth-century African American life."

memes.label: "Susan Graves"
memes.description: "A member of the distinguished Graves family of Gravestown, New Jersey, Miss Susan Graves is a housekeeper who, after the death of Meriwether Bye, takes in his son Peter Bye and raises him with family pride and practical wisdom."

memes.label: "Sylvia Marshall"
memes.description: "Joanna Marshall's elder sister in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, known for her quick wit, beauty, and stylishness. She marries Brian Spencer and becomes a mother of two."

memes.label: "The Teaching Profession in There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "Teaching as career path, community service, and vehicle of racial uplift in Jessie Redmon Fauset's There Is Confusion."

memes.label: Tenderloin District, New York City
memes.description: A historically notorious neighborhood in Manhattan where Maggie Ellersley grew up in a tenement on Thirty-fifth Street, described as a formative influence on her character and her determination to escape poverty.

memes.label: The Dance in There Is Confusion
memes.description: Dance functions in Jessie Redmon Fauset's *There Is Confusion* as both a specific social practice and a broader metaphor — Joanna Marshall's gift for dance embodies the novel's themes of grace, self-expression, and the tension between artistic ambition and racial expectation in early twentieth-century Black middle-class life.

relations.label: Racial Ambition in There Is Confusion

memes.label: "Tom Mason"
memes.description: "A successful violinist and orchestra leader in Philadelphia in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, who employs Peter Bye as an accompanist and represents both economic opportunity and the temptation of abandoning ambition for comfort."

memes.label: "Vera Manning"
memes.description: "A member of Joanna Marshall's old dancing-class in Jessie Redmon Fauset's 1924 novel There Is Confusion, who chooses to pass for white in New York City, abandoning her Harlem community and her romance with Harley Alexander in pursuit of escape from racial constraints."

memes.label: Washington, D.C.
memes.description: A significant secondary setting in Jessie Redmon Fauset's *There Is Confusion* — a city that represents both the aspirational horizons of Black middle-class life and a contrasting social culture to Philadelphia, where "strikingly dressed" strivers place appearance above substance.

memes.label: "Wealth and Economic Aspiration in There Is Confusion"
memes.description: "The role of wealth, economic aspiration, property ownership, and class distinction in Jessie Redmon Fauset's There Is Confusion."

memes.label: World War I in There Is Confusion
memes.description: The First World War (1914–1918) serves as a pivotal backdrop in *There Is Confusion*, affecting major characters' lives through military service, family legacy, and post-war racial dynamics in Harlem and Philadelphia.
